name: Gooseberry Custard (English)
recipeIngredient: 2 lbs Gooseberries, washed and topped and tailed
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the gooseberries and drain well.
2. Place a saucepan with water over medium heat and bring to a simmer.
3. Add the gooseberries to the simmering water and cook until softened, about 10-15 minutes.
4. Remove from heat and gently mash the gooseberries using a fork.
5. Stir in the butter, rosewater or lemon juice, and sugar. Cook for a few minutes over low heat until the sugar is completely dissolved.
6. Whisk together the double cream and eggs in a separate bowl.
7. Gradually stir the egg mixture into the mashed gooseberry mixture.
8. Return the mixture to the saucepan and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until thickened.
9. Pour into serving bowls or individual ramekins.
10. Chill in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour before serving.
11. Serve with biscuits such as boudoir or cats' tongue.
This very old recipe is a precursor to the more modern Gooseberry Fool. 'Custard' was the usual Suffolk description of a creamy dessert or pudding.
